name: Incline Dumbbell Front Raise
resistance_profile: Reclining on an incline bench lets the arms hang behind the torso at the bottom, loading the front delt in a deep stretch and biasing the lengthened position.
cues: Let the arms hang behind you at the bottom for a full stretch
Raise to shoulder height with a fixed elbow angle
Control the eccentric back into the stretch
execution_steps: Set a bench to a 45-60 degree incline and sit back against it, chest up, a dumbbell in each hand.
Let the arms hang down and slightly behind the torso so the front delts feel a stretch.
With a slight elbow bend, raise the dumbbells forward and up to about shoulder height.
Lower under control back into the stretched position and repeat.
good_when: You want a loaded stretch the standing raise cannot provide
Adding lengthened-biased isolation for the front delt
Building shoulder hypertrophy with lighter, controlled load
bad_when: Reaching behind the body aggravates the shoulder
You only need basic pressing volume
You cannot control the weight in the stretched position
common_errors: Cutting the stretch short at the bottom
Going too heavy and losing control at the stretch
progressions: Add a pause in the stretched position
regressions: Reduce the incline angle and the load
